title: Parsing PDFs & Multi-Column Layouts
concept: Layout-aware extraction that respects reading order instead of naive left-to-right text extraction.

exercises.prompt: In the last lesson, how did we make sure that images and diagrams in documents could be understood by an LLM?

options.text: By deleting the images to save storage space
By generating text descriptions or captions for the diagrams
By converting all surrounding text into images too

exercises.explain: In the previous lesson, we learned that generating descriptive text captions for images and diagrams is the key to making visual data searchable and readable by text-based LLMs.

cards.title: The PDF Jumble
cards.body: PDFs look beautiful to humans, but to computers, they are just a chaotic map of characters scattered on a canvas. If you copy-paste text from a two-column paper, you often get a jumbled mess where sentences from different columns collide.

cards.title: Layout-Aware Extraction
cards.body: Layout-aware extraction is a smart parsing technique that identifies structural elements like columns, headers, and footers. Instead of reading text blindly across the page, it reconstructs the natural human reading order.

cards.title: The Newspaper Column Trap
cards.body: Imagine reading a two-column newspaper. If you read straight across from left-to-right, the sentences from column one and column two mix together into gibberish. Layout-aware parsing is like having a human reader who follows each column down to the bottom before moving to the next.

cards.title: Parsing a Split Page
cards.body: If a PDF has a left column about 'Apples' and a right column about 'Oranges', a naive parser reads: 'Apples are red Oranges are orange and sweet and round'. A layout-aware parser extracts 'Apples are red and round' followed by 'Oranges are orange and sweet'.

cards.title: The Coordinates Myth
cards.body: Many developers believe that all text extractors read PDFs exactly as humans see them. In reality, standard libraries like basic PyPDF extract text strictly by coordinate position, which completely scrambles multi-column layouts and tables.

cards.title: Horizontal vs. Vertical Flow
cards.body: See how naive extraction reads straight across, while layout-aware extraction follows the logical flow.

left.text: Reads straight across: 'Left Col 1 Right Col 1 Left Col 2 Right Col 2'

right.text: Reads down columns: 'Left Col 1 Left Col 2' then 'Right Col 1 Right Col 2'

exercises.prompt: What happens when you use a naive left-to-right text extractor on a two-column PDF report?

options.text: It skips the second column entirely
It merges sentences from both columns horizontally, scrambling the meaning
It converts the document into a spreadsheet format
It highlights the columns in different colors

exercises.explain: Naive extraction reads coordinates from left to right across the entire horizontal line, which merges parallel columns together.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Layout-aware extraction helps keep headers and footers from interrupting the main body paragraphs.
exercises.explain: Layout-aware tools are designed specifically to recognize boundaries, headers, footers, and column flows to keep the text in order.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the rule for handling multi-column documents:
exercises.sentence: To prevent text from different columns mixing together, you must preserve the original reading order during extraction.
exercises.explain: Maintaining the correct reading order is essential because it keeps sentences and paragraphs coherent for the LLM.

exercises.prompt: Identify the text output that was likely generated by a naive (non-layout-aware) PDF reader:

options.text: Python is a versatile programming language. SQL is used for database queries.
We analyzed the sales data. The results showed a 10% increase in revenue.
Python is a SQL is used versatile language for queries.

exercises.explain: Option C shows a typical multi-column parsing failure where the system read horizontally across columns (mixing the topics of Python and SQL), while the others are coherent.

exercises.prompt: You are building a research assistant bot that reads two-column scientific papers. Why does layout-aware extraction prevent the LLM from generating incorrect summaries?

options.text: It converts all page text into a single high-resolution image first
It groups text blocks into columns and reads vertically before moving to the next column
It ignores images and tables to speed up the text extraction process
It automatically translates multi-column text into different languages

exercises.explain: Reading across columns horizontally (naive extraction) mixes unrelated text, causing the LLM to process scrambled instructions. Layout-aware parsers respect the vertical flow of each column separately, ensuring the instructions remain in the correct logical order.
